This baton is about a foot long and contains a single spell. The appearance typically relates to the spell within.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell at the indicated level.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of the spell at the listed rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Continuation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3051)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=731" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 282

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand increases a spell’s duration. Yellow embers spiral over its surface until the spell ends.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and its duration is increased by half.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" /> and a duration no less than 10 minutes and no greater than 1 hour.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and its duration is increased by half.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" /> and a duration no less than 10 minutes and no greater than 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Shardstorm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3052)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=610" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=731" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 283

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand features a carved dragon’s head at its top and a polished metal sphere set in its midsection.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) of the indicated level. After you cast the spell, an additional missile or missiles are released from the wand at the start of each of your turns, as though you cast the 1-action version of _force barrage_. Choose targets each time. This lasts for 1 minute, until you’re no longer wielding the wand, or until you try to activate the wand again.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) of the appropriate rank.

Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet; add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller; or add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, can't have a duration, and must have an area of burst (10 feet or more), cone, or line.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accolade Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3054)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Although not all wizards have gone through formal training, it's become tradition to enchant robes representing the arduous training required and festoon them with honors one has earned. Typically, an _accolade robe_ is styled after a single wizard school, with appropriate colors and symbols. Wearing these robes grants a +2 item bonus to Arcana checks.

 The pockets of the robe tie to an extradimensional space that can hold 1 Bulk of items, none of which can have greater than light Bulk. The items must be related to spellcasting and academics—spellbooks, scrolls, wands, scholarly journals, and other academic supplies the GM allows. These items do not count against your Bulk limit. You can Interact to retrieve or stow items normally. 

**Activate—Review** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You retrieve an item of your choice from the robe's storage, then Recall Knowledge. 

**Activate—Extra Credit** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a school spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a wizard of the associated school.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Accolade Robe
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Accolade Robe (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284

**Price** 13,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Arcana checks increases to +3. You can activate the robe’s Review once per minute as a free action.
name: Accolade Robe

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone

Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Amplifying)

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Envisioning)

Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Nourishing)

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Preserving)

If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Sprouting)

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Repairing)

summary_markdown: This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone  invested , you gain a +1 …

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Crescent)
