Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Flickering)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Armbands of Athleticism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3057)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 285

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armbands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Skilled awl work has imprinted images of a muscled weightlifter into these tiered leather bands, which grant you enhanced stamina and skill when performing athletic exercises. While fastened to your upper arms, the armbands give you a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks. In addition, whenever you use an action to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) and you succeed at the Athletics check, add a +5-foot item bonus to the distance you move.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Armbands of Athleticism
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 285, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

**Price** 645 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Armbands of Athleticism (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 285, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

**Price** 13,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Athletics checks is +3, and the bonus to a successful check to Climb or Swim is +10 feet.
name: Armbands of Athleticism

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armbands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Decorated with clear gemstones, these thick metal bands spread an inflexible layer of force over your body. The force grants you a +1 item bonus to AC and saving throws, and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5 as armor. You can affix talismans to the bands as though they were light armor. 

**Activate—Return Force** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))  **Trigger** A creature critically misses you with a melee Strike.;  **Effect** You Shove the creature using the bands' Athletics modifier of +14.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bands of Force](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3058)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=610" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armbands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Decorated with clear gemstones, these thick metal bands spread an inflexible layer of force over your body. The force grants you a +1 item bonus to AC and saving throws, and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5 as armor. You can affix talismans to the bands as though they were light armor. 

**Activate—Return Force** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))  **Trigger** A creature critically misses you with a melee Strike.;  **Effect** You Shove the creature using the bands' Athletics modifier of +14.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boots of Bounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3059)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The springy soles of these sturdy leather boots cushion your feet and make each step lighter. These boots give you a +5- foot item bonus to your Speed and a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37). In addition, when you use the Leap action, you can move 5 feet further if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boots of Bounding
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 606

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boots of Bounding (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 606

**Price** 4,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Speed is +10 feet, and the bonus to High Jump and Long Jump is +3.
name: Boots of Bounding

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bracers of Missile Deflection](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3061)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracers

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bracers are made from plates of durable dawnsilver and gleam like the summer sun. 

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A ranged weapon attack hits you but doesn't critically hit; **Requirements** You are aware of the attack and not off-guard; **Effect** The bracers send the missile off-course. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If this would cause the attack to be a failure, the attack misses you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bracers of Missile Deflection
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 607

**Price** 52 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bracers of Missile Deflection (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 607

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You can activate the bracers once every 10 minutes.
name: Bracers of Missile Deflection

This nugget of polished tektite is trapped in a cage of braided wire and hangs from a silken cord. When wearing this amulet, you gain resistance 5 against damage from [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spells if you're living, or against [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spells if you're undead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bright red-and-gold cape is often interlaced with glittery threads and serves as a distraction. While wearing the cape, you gain a +2 item bonus to Deception checks. 

**Activate—Puff of Smoke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724). The space you leave and the one you appear in are filled with puffs of smoke that make anyone within concealed until they leave the smoke or the end of your next turn, at which point the smoke dissipates. Strong winds immediately disperse the smoke.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Charlatan's Cape

---

Tiny silver hooks decorate these fine silk gloves. They grant a +1 item bonus to Thievery and allow you to cast [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) as an innate occult spell. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Charlatan's Gloves
</title>

</traits>

right="Item 9"

Charlatan's Gloves (Greater)

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

The gloves' bonus increases to +2. If you are also wearing a _[charlatan's cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3063)_, whenever you would move an object using [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), you may instead have it disappear in a puff of smoke and reappear hovering in a space adjacent to yours. [Charm of Resistance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3065)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This charm, normally hung from the belt or worn around the neck, grants you resistance 5 against one type of energy damage: acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. Each charm is crafted to protect against a particular type of energy damage, and its design usually embodies the type of energy it protects the wearer from in some way. For instance, a _charm of cold resistance_ could be carved in the shape of a yeti, whereas a _charm of fire resistance_ would be made from volcanic glass.

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This platinum choker bears characters from a language's alphabet, and it gives knowledge of that language and the associated culture's customs. You gain a +1 item bonus to Society checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the chosen language. Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the flat check to perform an auditory action while deafened from 5 to 3.  

**Craft Requirements** You know the language or languages the choker grants.

</row>

---

When you pull up the hood of this nondescript gray cloak (an Interact action), you become drab and uninteresting, gaining a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks and to Deception checks to Impersonate a forgettable background character, such as a servant, but also taking a –1 item penalty to Diplomacy and Intimidation checks. 

**Activate—Cloak Identity** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull the cloak's hood up and gain the benefits of [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) for 1 hour or until you pull the hood back down, whichever comes first.

</column>

Animal claws are woven into the thick leather of these bracers. 

**Activate—Extend Claws** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104). [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=113)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The bracers fuse temporarily with your forearms, with the claws extending to your fingertips. You gain a climb Speed of 20 feet and a claw unarmed attack with the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) traits that deals 1d6 slashing damage. This lasts for 10 minutes or until you Dismiss it.

---

This cloak flows, covering and concealing the wearer's body. The cloak allows you to cast [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528) as an occult innate cantrip. Although naturally a dull gray, while invested the cloak picks up colors and patterns from its surroundings, granting a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks. 

**Activate—Draw Hood** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw the hood up and gain the effects of [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), with the spell's normal duration or until you pull the hood back down, whichever comes first.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Countering Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3070)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glittering charm is made of a gemstone flawed with a leaden inclusion. Spellcasters can cast spells into _countering charms_ that they've invested or that are invested by a willing creature. The spell's effect doesn't occur; the spell's power is instead stored within the charm, replacing any spell previously there. The charm can hold only spells cast from spell slots, not cantrips or focus spells. While the charm is invested, the creature who has invested it knows what spell is stored within and automatically identifies that spell when it's cast. 

**Activate—Counter** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))  **Trigger** You are targeted by or within the area of the spell stored within the charm; **Requirements** You have a free hand; **Effect** You present the charm and attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell, using the rank of the spell stored in the charm and a counteract modifier of +20. This expends the spell held in the charm.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Countering Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3070)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glittering charm is made of a gemstone flawed with a leaden inclusion. Spellcasters can cast spells into _countering charms_ that they've invested or that are invested by a willing creature. The spell's effect doesn't occur; the spell's power is instead stored within the charm, replacing any spell previously there. The charm can hold only spells cast from spell slots, not cantrips or focus spells. While the charm is invested, the creature who has invested it knows what spell is stored within and automatically identifies that spell when it's cast. 

**Activate—Counter** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))  **Trigger** You are targeted by or within the area of the spell stored within the charm; **Requirements** You have a free hand; **Effect** You present the charm and attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell, using the rank of the spell stored in the charm and a counteract modifier of +20. This expends the spell held in the charm.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Coyote Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3071)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dusty cloak is made of mangy brown-and-gray coyote fur. You gain a +1 item bonus to Survival checks. If you critically succeed at your Survival check to Subsist, you can feed twice as many additional creatures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Coyote Cloak
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Coyote Cloak (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The cloak grants a +2 item bonus, and if you critically succeed at a Survival check to Subsist, you can feed four times as many additional creatures.
name: Coyote Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crafter's Eyepiece](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3072)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rugged metal eyepiece etched with square patterns is designed to be worn over a single eye. Twisting the lens reveals a faint three-dimensional outline of an item you plan to build or repair, with helpful labels on the component parts. You gain a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks. When you Repair an item, increase the Hit Points restored to 10 + 10 per proficiency rank on a success or 15 + 15 per proficiency rank on a critical success.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crafter's Eyepiece
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crafter's Eyepiece (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The eyepiece grants a +2 item bonus and can be activated. 

**Activate—Prototype** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You calibrate the eyepiece to have it cast a 5th-rank [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477) spell over the course of 1 minute to construct a temporary item.
name: Crafter's Eyepiece

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Usage** worn headwear --- An elegant accoutrement for a witch who has come into the higher echelons of power, a _crown of witchcraft_ typically looks like a garland of flowering twigs, a jeweled circlet, or a tall hat of fine fabric. You gain a +1 item bonus to Intimidation checks, and if you're a witch, you gain a +2 item bonus to your patron skill.
If you have a familiar, you can attach a small portion of the crown's material to your familiar, such as a strip of fabric from a hat tied around its tail or a sprig of natural material linked to its collar; the familiar doesn't need to invest the item itself. If you do this, your familiar gains the [tough pet](/Familiars.aspx?ID=37) ability as long as the crown is invested by you. **Activate—Defiant Hex** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a witch [hex](/Traits.aspx?ID=324) spell. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. **Craft Requirements** You are a witch. Crown of Witchcraft **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Price** 1,000 gp Crown of Witchcraft (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Price** 21,000 gp --- The bonus to Intimidation checks is +2 and the bonus to your patron skill is +3.
name: Crown of Witchcraft

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"

>
[Dancing Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3074)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long and billowing scarf is typically woven of silk or sheer fabric and adorned with bells or other jangling bits of shiny metal. It grants a +1 item bonus to Performance checks to dance. 

**Activate—Swirling Scarf** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))   **Requirements** On your most recent action, you succeeded at a Performance check to dance; **Effect** You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the beginning of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dancing Scarf
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dancing Scarf (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The scarf grants a +2 bonus. When you activate the scarf, you can also Stride up to half your Speed or Step.
name: Dancing Scarf

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Usage** worn shoes **Bulk** L --- These brightly colored, soft-soled boots motivate you to perform risky stunts and grant you the agility to succeed. The boots grant you a +2 item bonus to Acrobatics checks and a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to Tumble Through an enemy's space.
The boots can grip solid surfaces and help you avoid a fall, allowing you to use the Grab an Edge reaction even if your hands aren't free. You treat falls as 10 feet shorter or, if you have the Cat Fall feat, treat your proficiency rank in Acrobatics as one degree better to determine the benefits of that feat. If you have Cat Fall and are already legendary in Acrobatics, you can choose the speed of your fall, from 60 feet per round up to normal falling speed. Daredevil Boots **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609 **Price** 900 gp Daredevil Boots (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609 **Price** 14,000 gp --- The bonus to Acrobatics checks is +3, and the bonus to Tumble Through is +2. The boots can be activated. **Activate—Daredevil Impulse** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The boots cast [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) on you.
name: Daredevil Boots

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devoted Vestments](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3077)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These vestments are made of panels showing various scenes from the legends of a particular deity. It serves as a religious symbol of that deity, and you gain a +2 item bonus to Religion checks. When you cast [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) or [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), healing granted to followers of that deity is increased by the rank of the spell. 

**Activate—Domain Devotion** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a cleric domain spell for a domain belonging to the deity the vestments are dedicated to. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a cleric who worships the deity tied to the vestments.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Devoted Vestments

[Diplomat's Badge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3078)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When displayed prominently, this brass badge makes creatures find you more agreeable. You gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks. 

**Activate—Diplomat's Bearing** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Attempt a DC 20 check to Recall Knowledge about people of a human ethnicity, a non- human ancestry, or some other type of creature. (The GM determines what your options are.) If you succeed, the badge's bonus increases to +2 for Diplomacy checks with creatures of that group for the rest of the day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diplomat's Badge

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289 **Usage** worn --- This item consists of two magically linked rings: an intricate, gleaming golden ring with a square-cut ruby, and a thick, plain iron ring. When you wield a melee weapon in the hand wearing the golden ring, the weapon's fundamental runes are replicated onto any melee weapon you wield in the hand wearing the iron ring. (The fundamental runes are weapon potency and striking, which add an item bonus to attack rolls and extra weapon damage dice, respectively.) Any fundamental runes on the weapon in the hand wearing the iron ring are suppressed.
The replication functions only if you wear both rings, and it ends as soon as you cease wielding a melee weapon in one of your hands. Consequently, the benefit doesn't apply to thrown attacks or if you're holding a weapon but not wielding it (such as holding in one hand a weapon that requires two hands to wield). Doubling Rings **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609 **Price** 50 gp Doubling Rings (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609 **Price** 1,300 gp --- The rings also replicate property runes from the weapon in the gold-ringed hand, so long as the weapon in the iron- ringed hand meets all the prerequisites for a given rune and is not a specific weapon. The weapon in the iron-ringed hand gains the benefits of those runes. All its own runes are suppressed. When you invest the rings, you can elect for the rings to transfer only fundamental runes, in which case they function as standard _doubling rings_.
name: Doubling Rings

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289 **Price** 15,000 gp **Usage** worn eyepiece --- When tied over your eyes, this ragged strip of black linen gives you darkvision and a +3 item bonus to Intimidation checks. You can see through the blindfold, but only using darkvision.
The first time a particular creature sees you in a day, it must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or be [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1. This is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect, and your allies become immune to it after a week. **Activate—Visions of Terror** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You damage a creature with a Strike; **Effect** Your target is gripped by intense fear. This has the effect of a DC 37 [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) spell. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
name: Dread Blindfold

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Earthglide Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3081)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brown-and-gold cloak covers you from head to toe. Its weighty fabric doesn't move with the wind, instead hanging still as if carved of stone. 

**Activate—Glide Through Earth** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You Burrow through dirt and stone up to your land Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of your passing. If you end your movement inside solid stone, you are forcibly expelled into the nearest open area, taking 1d6 bludgeoning damage for every 5 feet between the end of your movement and the open area. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Earthglide Cloak

    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Entertainer's Cincture](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3082)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The designs adorning these lush sashes often imitate the decor of famous opera houses, theaters, and museums. When you invest this item, choose Deception, Diplomacy, Intimidation, or Performance; you gain a +2 item bonus to that skill. 

**Activate—Encore** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a bard composition spell. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. 

**Activate—Transcribe** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tuck a small roll of paper into the cincture. For the next 10 minutes or until you Dismiss the activation, any performance you make is recorded on the paper, and the paper expands as necessary to accommodate it. Depending on the type of performance, this might take the form of sheet music, a transcript, or a diagram of dance moves.

**Craft Requirements** You are a bard.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eye of Fortune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3083)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289

**Price** 2,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Adherents of [Erastil](/Deities.aspx?ID=6), god of the hunt, create these magical eye patches. An eye of fortune has a jeweled eye symbol on its front, allowing you to magically see through the eye patch as though it were transparent. 

**Activate—Luck Beyond Sight** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76))
  **Trigger** You attack a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) creature and haven't attempted the flat check yet;  **Effect** You can roll the flat check for the concealed or hidden condition twice and use the higher result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eye of Fortune

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Price** 1,250 gp **Usage** worn collar **Bulk** L --- This engraved duskwood gorget seems to vibrate with ferocity, granting you a +2 item bonus to Intimidation checks. **Activate—Primal Roar** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once during the duration of each polymorph effect; **Requirements** You're in a non-humanoid form via a polymorph effect; **Effect** You unleash a bestial roar, attempting a single Intimidation check compared to the Will DCs of all enemies within 30 feet to impose the effects below. **Critical Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 2
**Success** The creature is frightened 1
**Failure** The creature is unaffected
name: Gorget of the Primal Roar

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Handwraps of Mighty Blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

As you invest these embroidered strips of cloth, you must meditate and slowly wrap them around your hands. These handwraps have weapon runes etched into them to give your unarmed attacks the benefits of those runes, making your unarmed attacks work like magic weapons. For example_, +1 striking handwraps of mighty blows_ would give you a +1 item bonus to attack rolls with your unarmed attacks and increase the damage of your unarmed attacks from one weapon die to two (normally 2d4 instead of 1d4, but if your fists have a different weapon damage die or you have other unarmed attacks, use two of that die size instead).

 You can upgrade, add, and transfer runes to and from the handwraps just as you would for a weapon, and you can attach talismans to the handwraps. Treat the handwraps as melee weapons of the brawling group with light Bulk for these purposes. Property runes apply only when they would be applicable to the unarmed attack you're using. For example, a property that must be applied to a slashing weapon wouldn't function when you attacked with a fist, but you would gain its benefits if you attacked with a claw or some other slashing unarmed attack.

 The entries below list the most typical combinations of fundamental runes.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Handwraps of Mighty Blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

As you invest these embroidered strips of cloth, you must meditate and slowly wrap them around your hands. These handwraps have weapon runes etched into them to give your unarmed attacks the benefits of those runes, making your unarmed attacks work like magic weapons. For example_, +1 striking handwraps of mighty blows_ would give you a +1 item bonus to attack rolls with your unarmed attacks and increase the damage of your unarmed attacks from one weapon die to two (normally 2d4 instead of 1d4, but if your fists have a different weapon damage die or you have other unarmed attacks, use two of that die size instead).

 You can upgrade, add, and transfer runes to and from the handwraps just as you would for a weapon, and you can attach talismans to the handwraps. Treat the handwraps as melee weapons of the brawling group with light Bulk for these purposes. Property runes apply only when they would be applicable to the unarmed attack you're using. For example, a property that must be applied to a slashing weapon wouldn't function when you attacked with a fist, but you would gain its benefits if you attacked with a claw or some other slashing unarmed attack.

 The entries below list the most typical combinations of fundamental runes.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Healer's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3087)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These clean, white gloves never show signs of blood, even after they're used to stitch up wounds or treat other ailments. They give you a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks. 

**Activate—Healer's Touch** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You soothe the wounds of a willing, living, adjacent creature, restoring 2d6+7 Hit Points to that creature. This is a healing vitality effect. You can't harm undead with this healing. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Healer's Gloves
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 612

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Healer's Gloves (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 612

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The gloves provide a +2 bonus and restore 4d6+15 Hit Points.
name: Healer's Gloves

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hellfire Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3088)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These heavy boots are made of blackened metal and always feel warm to the touch, with streams of glowing embers cascading off their heels. While wearing hellfire boots, you gain resistance 10 to fire damage. 

**Activate—Devil's Dance** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You Stride. Each square you move through during your Stride is scorched with hellish flames, becoming hazardous terrain for 1 minute. A creature that moves through one of these spaces takes 3d6 fire damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hellfire Boots

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Usage** worn --- Fine silk lines this fashionable pocket, which is typically cinched to a belt or tailored into a piece of formal clothing. The pocket can hold no more than one item of light Bulk, plus incidental items of negligible Bulk. The pocket grants you a +2 item bonus to Society and to Stealth checks to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) in the pocket.
The pocket can produce stationery and writing implements of high quality. When you [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=60), you can use the pocket to produce ideal materials to make the forgery. Any materials must be able to fit through the opening of the pocket, such as a roll of parchment, an inkwell, or a loupe. Though they're excellent tools, they don't have any value if sold and disappear once their function in making a forgery is fulfilled. **Activate—Papers Please** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You create a temporary forgery by imagining the document you need and pulling it from the pocket. Attempt to [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=60) of the document you desire, with the GM rolling the secret check as normal. Its quality is based on your check, but the document disintegrates after 1 hour. Humbug Pocket **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Price** 650 gp Humbug Pocket (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Price** 12,500 gp --- The item bonus is +3. You can forge up to five documents with Papers Please, but they must serve a similar purpose— five invitations to the same party for different attendees, for example. Attempt only one check for all the documents.
name: Humbug Pocket

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 291 **Price** 19,000 gp **Usage** worn garment **Bulk** 2 --- This strange and intricate harness fits snugly to the torso. Once you invest the apparatus, numerous artificial limbs with various tools, clamps, and lenses whirl into action, following your mental commands effortlessly.
When using this apparatus, you gain a +3 item bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43), [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23), and [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42), and you reduce the minimum time required to Craft an item to 1 day. If you succeed at your Crafting check and spend more downtime to continue work on the item after the minimum number of days, each day you spend reduces the remaining raw material cost by an amount based on your level + 1 and your proficiency rank in Crafting; on a critical success, each day reduces the remaining raw material cost by an amount based on your level + 2 and your proficiency rank. If you are 20th level, on a critical success your progress is 50 gp, 100 gp, 200 gp, or 350 gp for trained, expert, master, or legendary proficiency, respectively. **Activate—Inexplicable Patch** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You command the apparatus to magically jury-rig an item you hold or that's within 5 feet of you. The item is repaired, as a 3rd-rank [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) spell. This lasts for 10 minutes, after which the item returns to its previous state of disrepair unless you've Repaired it before then.
name: Inexplicable Apparatus

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lifting Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3091)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide leather belt grants you a +1 item bonus to Athletics checks and increases the amount you can easily carry. You can carry Bulk equal to 6 + your Strength modifier before becoming encumbered, and you can hold and carry a total Bulk up to 11 + your Strength modifier. 

**Activate—Assisted Lift** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You lift an object of up to 8 Bulk as though it were weightless. This requires two hands, and if the object is locked or otherwise held in place, you can attempt to Force it Open using Athletics as part of this activation. The object still has its full weight and Bulk for all other purposes—you just ignore that weight. The effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lifting Belt

[Living Mantle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3092)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The base of this cloak is a thick layer of moss, but it slowly picks up native plants from each area it spends time in. You gain a +2 item bonus to Nature checks. You also suffer no effects from severe cold and severe heat. 

**Activate—Druidic Secrets** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast an order spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a druid.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Living Mantle (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lover's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3093)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=590" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These white silk gloves are adorned in red hearts that glow faintly whenever you are adjacent to someone you feel particularly strongly toward. They buoy your spirit, giving you a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks. 

**Activate—Bond** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grasp the hands of a willing creature you have strong positive feelings about, regardless of the nature of those feelings. The creature gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws and 10 temporary Hit Points for 10 minutes. If the creature shares your feelings, you gain the same benefits, and for the duration, when you both roll a success on a saving throw against an emotion effect that causes negative emotions, you both get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lover's Gloves

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mage's Hat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3094)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hat comes in many forms, such as a colorful turban or a pointy hat with a brim, and is adorned with symbols or runes. It grants you a +1 item bonus to Arcana checks and allows you to cast the [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) cantrip as an arcane innate cantrip.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mage's Hat
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mage's Hat (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This larger, fancier hat grants a +2 bonus and can be activated. Each greater mage's hat has a specific 4th-rank summon spell from the arcane list woven into its fabric, typically [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) or [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320). If you prepare arcane spells, you can change the spell to a different 4th-rank arcane summon spell you know when you invest it. 

**Activate—Hat Spell** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You doff the hat, causing magical energy to pour from it. You cast the spell stored in the hat.
name: Mage's Hat

This larger, fancier hat grants a +2 bonus and can be activated. Each greater mage's hat has a specific 4th-rank summon spell from the arcane list woven into its fabric, typically [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) or [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320). If you prepare arcane spells, you can change the spell to a different 4th-rank arcane summon spell you know when you invest it. 

**Activate—Hat Spell** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You doff the hat, causing magical energy to pour from it. You cast the spell stored in the hat.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Masquerade Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3095)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This delicately embroidered scarf matches with every outfit and can even complete a costume or disguise with illusions. 

**Activate—Masquerade** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You arrange the scarf over your lower face, and it casts a 1st-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) spell on you, which ends immediately if the scarf is removed. You can alter the scarf's appearance or make it invisible as part of the _illusory disguise_, but it can still be felt if touched.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Masquerade Scarf
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Masquerade Scarf (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The activation is a 2-action activity, you can activate it any number of times per day, and the [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) is 2nd rank.
name: Masquerade Scarf

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Messenger's Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3096)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silver signet ring changes to match the insignia of a leader or organization you serve (or your own face, if you serve no one else). It grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks and lets you cast [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) as an arcane innate spell at will. 

**Activate—Dispatch Messenger** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ring casts [_animal messenger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441) to your specification. The animal is a magical creature that springs from the ring, and its appearance suits the iconography or heraldry of the leader or organization represented by the ring.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Messenger's Ring
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 613

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Messenger's Ring (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 613

**Price** 13,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ring grants a +3 bonus and can be activated in an additional way. 

**Activate—Sending** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The ring casts [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) to your specifications.
name: Messenger's Ring

The sleek, black lenses of these goggles somehow make everything seem more brightly lit. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight. 

**Activate—Darkvision** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Adjusting your goggles, you gain darkvision for 1 hour.

</title>

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This amulet is hollow and shaped in the form of an unblinking eye. Its cavity typically holds some fragment of occult text. While wearing the pendant, you gain a +1 item bonus to Occultism checks, and you can cast the guidance cantrip as an occult innate spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Pendant of the Occult
</title>

</traits>

right="Item 9"

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

The pendant grants a +2 item bonus and can be activated. 

**Activate—Dream Message** <actions string="" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast a 4th-rank [_dream message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=90) spell.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Persona Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3099)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Despite covering the entire face, this alabaster mask does not hinder vision or other senses. Wearing the mask grants a +1 item bonus to Performance checks while acting, orating, performing comedy, or singing. 

**Activate—Assume Role** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** You change the mask's appearance into an artistic rendition of a dramatic character of your choice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Persona Mask
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 613

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Persona Mask (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 613

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mask grants a +2 bonus and gains another activation. 

**Activate—Sacrifice Role** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76))
 **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail a Performance check that benefits from the mask's bonus;  **Effect** You change the mask's character and reroll the Performance check, using the second result.
name: Persona Mask

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Primeval Mistletoe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3100)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sprig of berry-festooned holly and mistletoe doesn't wilt or rot. It can be used as a primal locus, and it grants a +1 item bonus to Nature checks while you wear it. 

**Activate—Anoint** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You squeeze juice from one of the berries and smear it onto a weapon made primarily of wood to cast [_runic weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658) on it, or onto a creature to cast [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657) on it. 

**Activate—Bind** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the sprig, then a tree to cast [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) upon yourself, turning into a vine on the touched tree.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Primeval Mistletoe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3100)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sprig of berry-festooned holly and mistletoe doesn't wilt or rot. It can be used as a primal locus, and it grants a +1 item bonus to Nature checks while you wear it. 

**Activate—Anoint** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You squeeze juice from one of the berries and smear it onto a weapon made primarily of wood to cast [_runic weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658) on it, or onto a creature to cast [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657) on it. 

**Activate—Bind** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the sprig, then a tree to cast [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) upon yourself, turning into a vine on the touched tree.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Propulsive Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3101)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sleek red boots make your legs feel like they're bursting with energy. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your land Speed and to any climb or swim Speeds you have. 

**Activate—Quickening Stomp** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You stomp three times and gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) condition for 1 minute. You can use the extra action to Stride, Climb, or Swim. (You must still attempt an Athletics check for the Climb and Swim actions unless you have the appropriate movement type.)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Propulsive Boots

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Retrieval Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3102)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=596" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This belt is covered in small pouches that clasp with buttons of painstakingly carved stone. The belt is tied to an extradimensional space that can hold one item of 1 Bulk or less. Anyone holding the belt can sense its contents, but only those who've invested it can store or retrieve items. Many retrieval belts are found with an item already inside. 

**Activate—Store Item** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))   **Requirements** There is room for an item in the belt; **Effect** One item you're holding with a Bulk of 1 or less vanishes into the belt's extradimensional space. 

**Activate—Retrieve Item** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))   **Requirements** An item is stored in the belt and you have a free hand; **Effect** The item stored in the belt appears in your hand. Neither Store Item nor Retrieve Item can be activated again for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retrieval Belt
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retrieval Belt (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The belt can store up to three items at a time.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retrieval Belt (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The belt can store up to 10 items at a time.
name: Retrieval Belt

Claw-like prongs on this thick golden band bears extend to dig deep into sheer surfaces when you are Climbing. This ring grants you a climb Speed equal to half your land Speed. Penalties to your Speed (including those from your armor) apply before halving. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Climbing

This plain silver ring has an almost oily sheen. While wearing the ring, you gain a +2 item bonus to Deception checks. 

**Activate—Sweeten Lies** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Snapping your fingers on the hand that wears the ring causes the ring to cast [_honeyed words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558) on you with no visual manifestations of a spell being cast.

</column>

    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ring of Sigils](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3105)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silver band is carved with the personal sigils of different individuals, adding one to represent you when you invest it. The ring allows you to cast [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) as an arcane innate cantrip. 

**Activate—Track Sigil** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You detect the general direction toward the most recent _sigil_ you created using the ring. This activation fails if the _sigil_ is more than 5 miles away or if there's lead or running water between you and the _sigil_. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

<traits>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This polished wooden ring constantly refreshes your body and mind. You don't need to eat or drink while wearing it, and you need only 2 hours of sleep per day to gain the benefits of 8 hours of sleep. A _ring of sustenance_ doesn't function until it's been worn and invested continuously for a week. Removing it resets this interval.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Sustenance

This obsidian ring allows you to partially warp your spells through the Netherworld, allowing them to strike directly at a target's body. 

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513))    **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell that requires a spell attack roll against Armor Class, choose Fortitude DC or Reflex DC. You make your spell attack roll against that defense instead of AC. If the spell has multiple targets, the choice of DC applies to all of them.

    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shining Symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3109)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 295

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

If you worship a deity, this golden amulet transforms into your deity's religious symbol when you invest it. You gain a +1 item bonus to Religion. The symbol casts dim light in a 20-foot emanation. 

**Activate—Spiritual Light** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The light cast by the symbol becomes bright light for 10 minutes and shines through bodies to reveal hints of the spirits within. Creatures in the light receive a –1 status penalty to Deception and Stealth checks. You can Dismiss this activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Shining Symbol (Major)

---

The bonus to Religion is +3. Spiritual Light's penalty is –3, and while it's active your enemies in the light gain weakness 10 to spirit damage.

>
[Shining Symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3109)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 295

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

If you worship a deity, this golden amulet transforms into your deity's religious symbol when you invest it. You gain a +1 item bonus to Religion. The symbol casts dim light in a 20-foot emanation. 

**Activate—Spiritual Light** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The light cast by the symbol becomes bright light for 10 minutes and shines through bodies to reveal hints of the spirits within. Creatures in the light receive a –1 status penalty to Deception and Stealth checks. You can Dismiss this activation.

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296 **Usage** worn garment **Bulk** L --- This loose robe has wide, voluminous sleeves that each contain an extradimensional space. These spaces each function as a spacious pouch that can hold up to 5 Bulk of items (for a total of 10 Bulk), though no individual item can be of more than 1 Bulk; the sleeves grow slightly heavy as you reach maximum capacity. You can add or remove an item from a sleeve with a single hand free as an Interact action.
If a sleeve is completely empty, you can place your own familiar into that extradimensional space. It can survive comfortably in your sleeve for up to 1 hour, after which it begins to suffocate. While in your sleeve, it can't be affected or targeted by any effects, but you don't benefit from any master abilities. A familiar can exit the sleeve of its own volition with a single action that has the manipulate and move traits. You can't place any other creature into your sleeves, nor can you place your familiar in a sleeve if it's larger than Tiny. If your familiar is in your sleeve, you can't place any items in the sleeve. Sleeves of Storage **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263 **Price** 100 gp Sleeves of Storage (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263 **Price** 600 gp --- Each sleeve can hold up to 20 Bulk of items, and a familiar can survive within a sleeve for up to 4 hours.
name: Sleeves of Storage

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Symbol of Conflict](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3111)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tarnished necklace can be attuned only by someone who is holy or unholy. When you attune it, it transforms into your deity's religious symbol or a personal symbol if you don't have a deity. You receive a +1 item bonus to Religion and a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against holy and unholy effects. 

**Activate—Presence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The symbol casts [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18) or [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25). The counteract DC of these effects is 18, and the counteract modifier is +8.

**Craft Requirements**  You must be holy or unholy.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Symbol of Conflict
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Symbol of Conflict (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Religion is +2. Change the list of spells to 4th- rank [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), and [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467). The DC is 27 and the counteract modifier is +17.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Symbol of Conflict (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Religion is +3. Change the list of spells to 6th- rank bane, bless, divine wrath, and cleanse affliction. Add 6th-rank [_summon celestial_](/Spells.aspx?ID=317) to this list if you are holy and 6th- rank [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323) if you are unholy. The DC is 38 and the counteract modifier is +28.
name: Symbol of Conflict

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296 **Price** 40,000 gp **Usage** worn --- When invested, this ornate crown and its incandescent gemstone meld into your head and take the form of a tattoo. This grants you otherworldly sight and allows you to read auras. No one but you can manipulate the _third eye_ while it's invested by you. Your heightened senses and ability to sense emotional auras grant you a +3 item bonus to Perception checks.
You continuously see magic auras, as a 9th-rank [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) spell, except you see the location of all auras within 30 feet, not just the strongest. If you use a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to study a creature you can see, you can perceive an aura that conveys knowledge of that creature's health, including all conditions and afflictions it has and an approximate percentage of its remaining Hit Points. **Activate—Truesight** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You gain the effects of an 8th-rank [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) spell.
name: Third Eye
search_markdown:

[Tracker's Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3113)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These lenses of forest-green glass are bound in rough leather stitched with crude twine. While wearing these goggles, you gain a +1 bonus to Survival checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66). If you fail a check to Track, you can try again after 30 minutes rather than an hour.

</column>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant copper ring has miniature images of songbirds engraved around its circumference. You gain a +1 item bonus to Deception checks. 

**Activate—Throw Voice** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Twisting the ring around your finger allows you to magically throw your voice, with the effects of a [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) spell (DC 19).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ventriloquist's Ring
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ventriloquist's Ring (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 296, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

**Price** 670 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ring grants a +2 bonus. When you activate the ring, you gain the effects of 2nd-rank [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (DC 27). You can activate the ring any number of times per day.
name: Ventriloquist's Ring
